{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Nkoyo Pharmaceuticals Limited is a Nigerian company founded over three decades ago as a simple open market drug trade outlet but has over the years evolved into a full-fledged pharmaceutical company run by a team of professionals with its corporate headquarter in Lagos, Nigeria.

Our focus is mainly on delivering quality pharmaceutical products to our customers, distributors and stakeholders within and outside the country. We specialize in the wholesale distribution of quality pharmaceutical products and operate as the sole distributor in Africa for major pharmaceutical production companies in India. We are a leading contract distributor for Pharmaceutical Tablets, Gels, Pain Relief Balms, Creams, Sex Enhancers etc. Our Products are manufactured at factories with WHO Certification and are distributed in more than 10 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Core Responsibilitie
Financial Record Keeping:


	Maintain accurate financial records (journals, ledgers, and accounts)
	Record daily financial transactions
	Ensure all entries are properly documented and classified.


Financial Reporting:


	Assist with preparing monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
	Generate reports such as income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements.


Tax Management:


	Prepare and file tax returns
	Ensure compliance with tax laws and regulations
	Calculate tax liabilities and ensure timely payments.


Accounts Payable &amp; Receivable Oversight:


	Supervise AP and AR processes
	Ensure timely payments to suppliers and collections from customers
	Monitor cash flow and working capital.


Reconciliation:


	Perform bank reconciliations
	Reconcile general ledger accounts
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies.


Audit Support:


	Prepare documents for internal and external audits
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and company policies
	Implement audit recommendations.


Internal Controls &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain and improve internal control systems
	Ensure adherence to financial policies and procedures
	Prevent fraud and financial mismanagement.


Typical KPIs (Performance Measures)


	Accuracy of financial reports
	Timeliness of reporting deadlines
	Compliance with tax and regulatory requirements
	Audit findings and error rates.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp; to: jobs@nkoyopharma.com using &quot;Accountant&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at Nkoyo Pharmaceuticals Limited




        
    

        
        Nkoyo Pharmaceuticals Limited is a Nigerian company founded over three decades ago as a simple open market drug trade outlet but has over the years evolved into a full-fledged pharmaceutical company run by a team of professionals with its corporate headquarter in Lagos, Nigeria.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our focus is mainly on delivering quality pharmaceutical products to our customers, distributors and stakeholders within and outside the country. We specialize in the wholesale distribution of quality pharmaceutical products and operate as the sole distributor in Africa for major pharmaceutical production companies in India. We are a leading contract distributor for Pharmaceutical Tablets, Gels, Pain Relief Balms, Creams, Sex Enhancers etc. Our Products are manufactured at factories with WHO Certification and are distributed in more than 10 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Core Responsibilitie
Financial Record Keeping:


	Maintain accurate financial records (journals, ledgers, and accounts)
	Record daily financial transactions
	Ensure all entries are properly documented and classified.


Financial Reporting:


	Assist with preparing monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
	Generate reports such as income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements.


Tax Management:


	Prepare and file tax returns
	Ensure compliance with tax laws and regulations
	Calculate tax liabilities and ensure timely payments.


Accounts Payable & Receivable Oversight:


	Supervise AP and AR processes
	Ensure timely payments to suppliers and collections from customers
	Monitor cash flow and working capital.


Reconciliation:


	Perform bank reconciliations
	Reconcile general ledger accounts
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies.


Audit Support:


	Prepare documents for internal and external audits
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and company policies
	Implement audit recommendations.


Internal Controls & Compliance:


	Maintain and improve internal control systems
	Ensure adherence to financial policies and procedures
	Prevent fraud and financial mismanagement.


Typical KPIs (Performance Measures)


	Accuracy of financial reports
	Timeliness of reporting deadlines
	Compliance with tax and regulatory requirements
	Audit findings and error rates.


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV  to: [email protected] using "Accountant" as the subject of the mail.
